name: FinnGen PheWeb (latest release)
institution: FinnGen Consortium (HUS, Univ. Helsinki, THL, biobanks)
license: Custom FinnGen data use terms; summary stats free for research use after registration
notes: Clinician-curated endpoints; gene/variant/phenotype search; also hosts historical releases (R5-R12) and specialty PheWebs (labvalues, kanta, userresults).

name: Risteys (FinnGen + FinRegistry endpoint browser)
notes: Endpoint-level statistics, case counts, longitudinal relationships, survival analysis and drug statistics. Not a GWAS browser (see FinnGen PheWeb / Risteys links out to PheWeb for GWAS).

name: Multiple Manhattan Plot (MMP)
license: FinnGen access tiers apply for FinnGen data; tool itself is open for custom GWAS comparison
notes: Side-by-side visual comparison of multiple GWAS scans (e.g., meta-analyses across biobanks); not a standalone summary-stats repository.

name: LAVAA (volcano plot PheWAS viewer)
notes: 'Lightweight Association Viewer Across Ailments' — volcano-plot PheWAS visualization; can import data from OpenGWAS and other sources; integrated into FinnGen from release 10 onward.

name: NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog
cohort: Curated from >45,000 published GWAS across thousands of cohorts
notes: Largest curated catalog of published variant-trait associations; >40,000 full summary statistics datasets; REST API and FTP bulk downloads; deposition portal for authors.

name: Open Targets Platform
institution: EMBL-EBI / Wellcome Sanger / GSK / other industry partners
cohort: Integrates GWAS Catalog, UK Biobank, FinnGen, gene-burden studies (Genebass, Regeneron, AZ PheWAS), ChEMBL, ClinVar, etc.
sample_size: Aggregates data across hundreds of cohorts/studies
license: Open access (CC0 for most integrated data)
notes: Target-disease evidence integration for drug discovery; GraphQL API; downloadable Parquet/JSON datasets; fine-mapped credible sets via Gentropy.

name: Open Targets Genetics (legacy, merged into Platform)
last_updated: Functionality merged into Open Targets Platform (Gentropy)
notes: Locus-to-gene prioritisation and colocalisation; functionality has been folded into the main Open Targets Platform / Gentropy pipeline — kept here for historical reference.

name: AstraZeneca PheWAS Portal
notes: Gene-level collapsing/burden PheWAS on UK Biobank exomes; feeds evidence into Open Targets Platform gene-burden datasource.

name: Genebass
license: Open access; results from Karczewski et al. 2022
notes: Gene-based association summary statistics (exome-wide, ~4,000+ phenotypes); Hail-based backend; bulk downloads via Google Cloud.

name: Pan-UK Biobank (Pan-UKB)
institution: Broad Institute (Neale Lab / Atkinson / Martin labs)
ancestry: 6 continental ancestry groups (EUR, AFR, AMR, EAS, SAS, MID)
notes: Multi-ancestry GWAS across 7,221+ UK Biobank phenotypes; 16,131 GWAS + meta-analyses; downloadable via Hail MatrixTable / flat files on AWS Open Data (registry.opendata.aws/broad-pan-ukb).

name: Neale Lab UK Biobank GWAS (rounds 1-2, historical)
notes: Original European-ancestry-only UKB GWAS release (4,203 phenotypes); largely superseded by Pan-UKB but still widely cited/used.

name: BioBank Japan PheWeb (PheWeb.jp)
institution: BioBank Japan (Univ. of Tokyo IMSUT) / RIKEN IMS / Osaka University
notes: PheWeb-platform browser for BBJ GWAS; fine-mapping and Japanese-specific coding variant associations (Koyama et al. 2024); full summary statistics downloadable.

name: JENGER (Japanese ENcyclopedia of GEnetic associations by Riken)
institution: RIKEN Center for Integrative Medical Sciences
cohort: BBJ + IMM, JPHC, J-MICC, TMM cohorts
license: Open access download for research use
notes: Direct summary-statistics download site (not an interactive browser); complements PheWeb.jp.

name: GWASLab / CTG Catalog (meta-index of sumstats resources)
cohort: Meta-index of biobanks and consortium GWAS
notes: Not a data portal itself — a curated meta-index of biobanks/cohorts and links to their sumstats resources; useful as a cross-reference when adding new portals to this catalog.

name: Million Veteran Program (MVP) PheWAS/GWAS summary results via dbGaP
ancestry: Multi-ancestry (EUR, AFR, AMR, EAS HARE groups)
license: Open, no application required for the 2024 PheWAS summary release (dbGaP accession phs001672); full individual-level data requires separate dbGaP application
notes: dbGaP accession phs001672; the 2024 PheWAS summary-statistics release is openly downloadable from the dbGaP FTP site with no application (Verma et al. 2024, NCBI Insights announcement); this entry refers specifically to that open summary-level resource, not the full individual-level MVP dataset.

name: All of Us Data Browser (Public Tier)
ancestry: Highly diverse, majority historically underrepresented in biomedical research
license: Public Tier is fully open, no account needed; individual-level Researcher Workbench access requires free registration + training
notes: The public Data Browser (linked here) shows aggregate variant/phenotype summaries with no login required. Full individual-level WGS/array data lives in the separate Researcher Workbench, which is free but requires registration and Responsible Conduct of Research training — not included in the public-only scope of this catalog.

name: Type 2 Diabetes Knowledge Portal (T2DKP) / CMDKP
cohort: Aggregates 380+ GWAS datasets across T2D/metabolic cohorts
notes: Sister portals under the Common Metabolic Diseases Knowledge Portal (CMDKP) family: CVDKP (cardiovascular), T2DKP, Sleep Disorder KP, KidneyKP, etc. — all share the HuGeAMP platform.

name: MRC IEU OpenGWAS (gwas.mrcieu.ac.uk / MR-Base)
institution: MRC Integrative Epidemiology Unit, University of Bristol
license: Open access (registration for API tokens; some datasets restricted)
notes: Standardised GWAS-VCF format; powers Mendelian randomization via TwoSampleMR/MR-Base; R (ieugwasr) and Python clients available.

name: CARTaGENE PheWeb
institution: CERC in Genomic Medicine (Univ. of Michigan / Univ. de Montréal)
ancestry: European (analysis restricted to EUR ancestry in this release)
notes: Regenie-based GWAS on the Québec CARTaGENE cohort; results flagged as not fully curated (open-beta caveat on site); built on open-source PheWeb framework.

name: FinMetSeq PheWeb
institution: University of Michigan (SPH) / University of Eastern Finland (METSIM)
notes: Metabolomics (Metabolon platform) GWAS PheWeb for the METSIM cohort; example of the broader Michigan 'pheweb.org' family of instances hosted by the Boehnke/Abecasis groups.

name: PheWeb.org instance directory (Univ. Michigan-hosted browsers)
institution: University of Michigan (Boehnke/Abecasis groups) and PheWeb open-source project
license: Varies per instance; PheWeb framework itself is open-source (MIT)
notes: PheWeb is the open-source framework underlying FinnGen, BBJ, CARTaGENE, FinMetSeq, and many other browsers in this catalog; this entry is the framework/hub page, not a single cohort.

name: Global Biobank Meta-analysis Initiative (GBMI) results browser
institution: GBMI (23+ biobanks consortium, coordinated via Broad/Massachusetts General Hospital)
cohort: 23 member biobanks (UKB, FinnGen, BBJ, MVP, and others)
notes: Publicly downloadable meta-analyses of 14 exemplar disease endpoints harmonised across 23 member biobanks; a genuinely public entry point even though several individual member biobanks (e.g. deCODE, HUNT, Taiwan Biobank) do not themselves offer public query portals.

name: GTEx Portal (Genotype-Tissue Expression)
license: Open access (dbGaP for restricted genotype data; most eQTL results open)
notes: Reference eQTL/sQTL resource across human tissues; not disease-GWAS but essential for variant-to-gene functional annotation and colocalization workflows.

name: UK Biobank Pharma Proteomics Project (UKB-PPP) pQTL browser
institution: UK Biobank / Regeneron / GSK / Pharma consortium (13 companies)
license: Open access summary statistics (Synapse/registered download); individual-level via UK Biobank application
last_updated: 2023 (Nature papers) + integrated into FinnGen PheWeb gene pages
notes: Plasma proteomic GWAS (pQTL) resource; results integrated into FinnGen PheWeb gene pages and Open Targets Platform.

name: EpiGraphDB
institution: MRC Integrative Epidemiology Unit, University of Bristol
cohort: Graph database integrating OpenGWAS, literature, and other epidemiological resources
notes: Knowledge-graph interface layered on top of OpenGWAS/MR-Base results, literature mining, and drug-target data; complements rather than replaces OpenGWAS.

name: DICE (Database of Immune Cell Expression, eQTLs and Epigenomics)
cohort: Healthy human donors, sorted immune cell subsets
ancestry: Not the primary axis (small reference cohort)
notes: Immune-cell-type-resolved eQTL resource, useful for interpreting immune-mediated disease GWAS loci alongside GTEx.

name: GWAS ATLAS (Complex Trait Genetics Lab)
institution: Complex Trait Genetics Lab, VU Amsterdam
cohort: Curated repository of thousands of GWAS summary statistics
notes: Includes genetic correlation matrices (MAGMA/LDSC-derived) across thousands of trait pairs; strong for cross-trait genetic architecture browsing.

name: PsychENCODE Knowledge Portal
cohort: Postmortem human brain tissue (multiple psychiatric-disease and control cohorts)
license: Open access (some raw data via Synapse/NDA controlled access)
notes: Brain-tissue multi-omic (eQTL/isoQTL/chromatin) resource for interpreting neuropsychiatric GWAS loci.

name: Cardiovascular Disease Knowledge Portal (CVDKP)
notes: Sibling portal to T2DKP on the shared HuGeAMP/CMDKP platform, focused on cardiovascular traits.

name: Psychiatric Genomics Consortium (PGC) Downloads
cohort: Meta-analyses across 100+ contributing cohorts (incl. iPSYCH, UKB, deCODE, FinnGen)
sample_size: Varies by disorder (largest analyses >1,000,000 combined with UKB/23andMe)
license: Open access (registration for some restricted-use sumstats)
notes: Direct-download repository (not an interactive browser) for psychiatric-disorder GWAS meta-analyses (schizophrenia, MDD, bipolar, ADHD, ASD, PTSD, etc.).
